title: NOC 14405 – Transportation Route and Crew Schedulers | GTR Immigration
description: Transportation Route and Crew Schedulers play a crucial role in keeping public transit systems, airlines, railways, and freight operations running efficiently by designing schedules, assigning crews, and optimizing routes across private and public transportation networks in Canada. Classified under NOC 14405, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating practical immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of public-transit networksGrowth in freight and e-commerce deliveryInfrastructure modernization projectsEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement potentialEmployer-supported recruitment
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Transportation Route and Crew Schedulers may perform some or all of the following:
Review passenger volumes, cargo loads, travel distances, and staffing availability
Design or modify schedules using specialized software
Factor in holidays, construction, and special events
Assign crews to routes and equipment
Compile operational reports on personnel hours, maintenance, and mileage
Prepare public service guides and timetable information
employment_requirements: Several years of experience in a transportation sector
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 4,500 new positions for Transportation Route and Crew Schedulers, while about 3,900 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Transportation Route and Crew Schedulers are in demand across Canada’s transportation and logistics sectors. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Municipal transit authoritiesTrucking, courier, and delivery firmsRailwaysAirlines and airportsGovernment transportation agencies
Annual salaries typically range between $32,800 and $73,800 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
